pi_items.source: Task description and env setup script 'install_jamovi.sh' which extracts it from the real bfi dataset.
Task description and export script verify this exact path.
Task description and export script specify this path.
The data generation script (extract_bfi_neuroticism.py) explicitly outputs columns N1-N5, and the task instructs creating N1_Binary from N1.
The data generation script outputs column N2, and the task instructs creating N2_Binary from N2.
The task explicitly states the threshold in the formula 'IF(N1 > 3, 1, 0)'.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real data derived from the 'bfi' dataset (psych R package). The setup script extracts Neuroticism items (N1-N5) to '/home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/NeuroticiIndex.csv'. Using a threshold > 3 for 'N1' and 'N2' yields a contingency table with discordant pairs 559 and 106. Independent calculation of the McNemar test yields an extremely small p-value (exact: ~3.34e-75, chi-squared: ~8.79e-69). Therefore, the reported p-value should be effectively 0 (e.g., < .001). All file paths, variables, and parameters in the metadata accurately match the environment configuration and task instructions.
